title: crocus biflorus subsp. alexandri
intro: Crocus biflorus subsp. alexandri, also known as Alexandrian crocus or spring crocus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Crocus. It is a perennial bulbous plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Crocus biflorus subsp. alexandri include reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as providing antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
